ogTitle: Wedge Island vs Weeroona Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wedge Island and Weeroona Island.
heading: Wedge Island vs Weeroona Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wedge Island, South Australia and Weeroona Island, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wedge Island has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Weeroona Island (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wedge Island or Weeroona Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wedge Island has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Weeroona Island scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wedge Island scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Weeroona Island scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wedge Island or Weeroona Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wedge Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
